summary: Classified the fresh PR inventory shard from the hydrated preflight artifact. No merge is recommended because none of the open PRs has a complete merge preflight with a passed Codex /review. One security-sensitive open PR is routed centrally; one closed security-sensitive linked PR is quarantined as historical security context. Useful, focused open contributor PRs are kept as repair/merge candidates for normal maintainer finalization.

repair_candidate.summary: Repair contributor PR #66685 by rebasing/resolving the dirty branch for the expired exec approval followup warning suppression, then rerun review and changed-surface validation before any merge decision.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(agents): suppress expired approval followup warnings
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## What Problem This Solves

Repairs contributor PR #66685 so the focused expired exec approval followup warning suppression can be finalized on the original branch.

## Why This Change Was Made

The hydrated preflight shows #66685 is narrow and useful, but currently dirty against `main`. The executor should rebase or resolve conflicts on the existing maintainer-editable branch, preserve @pfrederiksen's contribution trail, rerun review, and validate the changed surface before any merge action.

## User Impact

Expired or already-gone exec approval followup results should not create noisy warning logs when the existing approval-not-found classifier marks the error benign.

## Evidence

Source PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/66685
Linked context: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/66675
Planned validation: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-shared.test.ts`; `pnpm check:changed`
credit_notes: Repair strategy updates the existing maintainer-editable branch instead of replacing it.
